charon.api.list_ledgers.reason: Called from charon.service.app (api.list_ledgers()) but that call site doesn't count: service/app.py is loaded by uvicorn via the string 'charon.service.app:app' (see service/__main__.py), which is invisible to static AST reachability, so the whole module reads as unreachable and this call cascades as a false positive.

charon.api.show_config.reason: Same cascading false positive as charon.api.list_ledgers: called from charon.service.app (api.show_config()), a module only reachable via uvicorn's string-based ASGI load.

charon.capability.actuals.ActualRow.reason: Same module as charon.capability.actuals.ActualsLedger — same disposition, same evidence.

charon.capability.actuals.ActualsLedger.reason: Zero production callers (tests/test_actuals_ledger.py imports it but capability/scorecard.py is the wired replacement per the original hand-rolled audit; decompose_sizing.py:54 only comments on it historically).

charon.discover.discover_models.reason: Zero production callers (cli.py:329-339 wires discover.import_openrouter_models, not discover_models). Docstring frames discover_models as the PROPOSAL-1 Phase A full-refresh orchestrator — could be a still-wanted feature not yet CLI-exposed, or a superseded design now that import_openrouter_models covers the wired path. Needs a product-intent call, not a code-only call.

charon.discover.load_cost_map.reason: Same module/bucket as charon.discover.discover_models — zero production callers; same product-intent ambiguity.

charon.doctor.probe_handoff.reason: Module docstring/help text (doctor.py:116-140) literally states the intended CLI surface: "Run: charon doctor --handoff --backend-cmd-a ... --backend-cmd-b ...". Designed, tested (tests/test_handoff.py, tests/test_handoff_crossvendor.py), just never connected to cli.py doctor subcommand dispatch (confirmed: zero doctor.* references in cli.py).

charon.engine.reconcile.FindingKind.reason: Same module/bucket as the already-tracked charon.engine.reconcile.ReconcileFinding; whole module has zero production callers. Same open call: wire into the coordinator or remove.

charon.engine.reconcile.ReconcileFinding.reason: Part of the original hand-rolled dead-code audit (engine/reconcile.py + board.set_cert() flagged unwired). set_cert() itself is now test-referenced so the detector no longer flags it, but this dataclass has zero callers anywhere. Whether reconcile.py should be wired into the coordinator or removed is an open call, not made here.

charon.engine.reconcile.Severity.reason: Same module/bucket as charon.engine.reconcile.ReconcileFinding; same open call.

charon.engine.reconcile.reconcile_static.reason: Same module/bucket as charon.engine.reconcile.ReconcileFinding; same open call.

charon.engine.semantic_proof.Independenc.reason: Actually wired: tests/test_semantic_proof.py:8 imports it directly, and test_decompose_surface.py:15-16 imports the same symbols. The detector’s _resolve_call() (tools/_vendor/ksf_inert_code.py:283-303) only correctly resolves from X import Y; Y() where Y is a symbol, not from X import Y; Y.func() where Y is a submodule — so when reachability is derived from a path that does pass the detector (e.g. a direct tests/ import), the production symbol is marked reachable in the live detector run today. Entry tracks the future risk that removing the test-side import path will re-flag it; matches the existing keep-detector-false-positive-* family.

charon.engine.semantic_proof.compute_cer.reason: Same pattern as charon.engine.semantic_proof.IndependenceCertificate — actually called via the from .engine import semantic_proof; semantic_proof.compute_certificate(...) pattern, but the detector’s qualified-submodule import resolution builds a malformed candidate (see tools/_vendor/ksf_inert_code.py:283-303) that doesn’t match the true dotted name. Detector gap, not dead code.

charon.land.propose_pr.reason: Actually wired: src/charon/cli.py references it as a bare attribute value ('opener = pr_opener or land.propose_pr') rather than a direct call. Its sibling run_scanners is wired (land.py:41, 246, 257); benchmark_scanners is intentionally a separate, manually-invoked instrument.
